title: It’s a ‘race to the finish,’ but Syracuse STEAM school will open in September (photos)
summary: Syracuse STEAM High School, a $100 million regional high school focused on science, technology, engineering, arts, and math, is scheduled to open in September 2025 with 250 inaugural freshman students despite ongoing construction. The state-funded project (98% reimbursement) represents significant infrastructure investment in workforce development for the region's growing tech economy, with facilities designed to support nine academic concentrations including robotics, animation, and data analytics.
department_refs: Department of Facilities and Capital Planning

political_signal.description: Multi-level government leadership (Mayor, County Executive, School Superintendent) publicly endorsing and championing the STEAM school as economic development and workforce pipeline strategy aligned with regional tech sector growth.

processing_notes: Dollar amount extracted: $100 million (not $100 as initially detected)
Article focuses on facilities/capital project rather than CIO-specific IT systems or digital services
May relate to CIO priorities around tech workforce pipeline and regional competitiveness
Historic preservation element noted (122-year-old building, architect Archimedes Russell retained)
Project timeline: approved 2020 (Gov. Cuomo), originally fall 2021, delayed due to pandemic and cost increases, now opening September 2025
